race.name: Bealach Mór
race.tagline: Holy Grail of UK cycling climbs: closed-road assault on Bealach na Bà.

vitals.location: Shieldaig, Applecross Peninsula, Scottish Highlands, Scotland

climate.description: Typical May Scottish Highlands: cool, rainy, windy, variable; exposed to Atlantic weather on Bealach na Bà.

features: Closed Highland roads with rugged climbs
primarily paved ascent of Bealach na Bà

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 76. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: Closed-road loop from Shieldaig over Bealach na Bà to Applecross peninsula, looping with Torridon/Skye views back to finish.
course_description.signature_challenge: Bealach na Bà pass—UK's biggest road climb.
suffering_zones: Bealach na Bà: 9km, 632m gain, 7% avg/20% max.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Local Shieldaig/Applecross; book early for Highlands.

history.origin_story: Founded 2006 by Hands On Events; now Shieldaig Assoc./Jenny Graham (relaunched as The Bà 2025+).

biased_opinion.summary: A savage, scenic masterpiece that punches way above its modest distance—pure suffering rewarded with Highland majesty. Prestige 3 belies its 'Holy Grail' status among UK climbs.

final_verdict.one_liner: UK's ultimate road climb on closed roads: brutal, beautiful, bucket-list essential.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Bealach Mór delivers the Bealach na Bà—626m over 9km at 20% max—in a 72km charity loop through paradise. Remote, raw, and relentlessly rewarding; T2 tier hides its elite suffering.

citations.label: Our pick of the best cycling events to enter in 2026 - Stolen Goat
The UK's best sportives: these events will make 2026 a year to remember

youtube_data.search_query: Bealach Mór gran fondo cycling Shieldaig

videos.title: The World's Biggest Climb? | GCN Vs Alto de Letras
videos.description:
The Alto de Letras is one of the world's longest climbs, rolling for over 83km through the Colombian Andes to over 3000 meters and is a mythical cycling destination. Whilst in South America Si rode his bike up the mountain pass to find out what makes it so special, and why Colombia produces so many fantastic cyclists like Egan Bernal, Rigoberto Uran and Lucho Herrera. The climb itself might be a busy road, and if the altitude doesn't leave you gasping for air, the stunning scenery will take your breath away.... 

Check out the GCN App: 
https://gcn.eu/app

 Last chance to grab yourself a bargain in the GCN Outlet: https://gcn.eu/3kv
Subscribe to GCN: http://gcn.eu/SubscribeToGCN
Register your interest in the GCN Club: https://gcn.eu/3ku
#Cycling #GCN #WithMe 

What is the longest climb you have ever cycled up? Have you ever ridden in Colombia? Are there any other climbs you think we should come and ride? 

For all of our adventures and epic rides follow us on Komoot: https://gcn.eu/komoothr

Join our Facebook community: http://gcn.eu/FBCommunity 

If you enjoyed this video, make sure to give it a thumbs up and share it with your friends. 👍

If you'd like to contribute captions and video info in your language, here's the link - https://gcn.eu/3kt

videos.title: Riding The EPIC Fausto Coppi Gran Fondo.
videos.description:
This is my first ride into the real high mountains for years! 

The Fausto Coppi Gran Fondo is part of my build up for the Haute Route and at  177km in length and with over 4000 meters of ascent, it's not a route for the faint hearted. 

It's fair to say this one really pushed me and forced me to dig deep inside. The main climb of the day, the Colle Fauniera is an absolute beast that holds it own alongside any of the iconic and more famous climbs in Europe! 

Thanks for watching.

videos.title: Can We Survive A 4000m Himalayan Gravel Climb?!
videos.description:
We’ve come to one of the most unforgiving places in the world, the Himalayas. High altitudes, steep climbs, brutal temperatures, all this adds up to one mighty challenge for Hank and Simon. 3,500m of elevation in under 1 hour and a half, can they do it?

Useful Links: 
Visit the GCN Shop 👉 https://gcn.eu/shop

What challenges would you like to see us take on next? 👀 Let us know in the comment section below! 💬

videos.title: Bealach Mor 2015 - The UK's hardest Cycling sportive
videos.description: Bealach Mor 2015. A sportive with 9600 ft (3000m) of ascent starting in Kilochewe, and cycling for 90 miles going over Bealach na Ba which is Britain's biggest road climb at 2053 ft (626m) in 6 miles (10k)
videos.curation_reason: This is the only video specifically about the Bealach Mór race itself, featuring a full 40-minute ride-along of the event including the Bealach na Bà climb.

terrain_notes.text: Raw surfaces in places aren't great so the descent is going to be interesting. These descents we taken on today are super sketchy loads of Mega tight hair pins the surface in places isn't great there's damp on the roads.
Both Hank and I have dropped our tire pressure to the point where we're now having to go down Hills a little bit steadily is if we hit any rocks at speed we're going straight to the rim.

gear_mentions.text: Big squishy gravel ties but this is still bumpy is anything you just want to try and smooth out as much as possible. The beauty of Ronnie tubeless.

race_day_tips.text: I'm riding easy taking no risks trying to keep the bike UPR right. As we approach the top of the climb I noticed a water fountain at the side of the road and decide stop in the air and beat the quees at the feed station bottles refilled.

My jersey pockets are as full of clothing as they are of food and there is an awful lot of that in there as well because it's going to be a long day out. Starting in this sub tropical paradise with 30-degree temperatures and high humidity and ending up in all likelihood in extremely cold and potentially very rainy conditions on the summit.

additional_quotes.text: 2,000 Riders here to do this one International Event rides from all over the world not a bad venue for rest start. Myself in 20300 Riders from 36 different nations lead the spectacular Pat gamber kunio bang on 7 a.m.

This part of the himalayage probably hasn't seen the blue no definitely not. Route planning apps like commute have much detail on the area and on Strava there are just a handful of segments in a huge radius.

rider_intel.search_text: The provided transcripts do not contain information about the Bealach Mór (The Bà Sportive) race in Scotland. The transcripts cover three different cycling events: a Colombian climb called Alto de letras (81km climb with extreme temperature variations from 30-degree subtropical conditions to cold summit weather), a Himalayan gravel adventure in Nepal featuring technical terrain with sketchy descents requiring dropped tire pressures, and an Italian Grand Fondo with 4,000m of climbing over 177km. These races feature challenging climbs, technical descents with poor surface conditions, and require careful pacing strategies. However, none of these events relate to the Scottish Highlands race specified, so no actionable intelligence can be extracted for the Bealach Mór event from these particular video transcripts.
